title: Multi-gate review workflow: CODEOWNERS + PR checklist (editorial->legal->a11y->merge) + reviewers ledger
context: Know-Your-Rights requires a documented, enforced multi-gate review workflow for all content PRs. The gate order is: editorial review (plain language, neutrality, non-advice/UPL) then legal review (licensed attorney admitted in the jurisdiction) then accessibility review then maintainer merge. This workflow must be encoded in CODEOWNERS and a PR checklist template, and a reviewers ledger must record attorney credentials and consent. No content bypasses the legal gate. Depends on the editorial policy (task 001).
objective: Define and implement the multi-gate content review workflow: CODEOWNERS configuration enforcing gate order, a PR checklist template covering all gates, and a reviewers ledger format that records attorney credentials (bar admission, jurisdiction, consent to credit). The workflow must make it structurally impossible to merge rights-stating content without a recorded attorney sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: CODEOWNERS configured so that rights-stating content files require approval from the editorial reviewer role, the licensed-attorney reviewer role (admitted in the jurisdiction), and the accessibility reviewer role before the maintainer can merge

PR checklist template covers all four gates in order: (1) editorial — plain language, reading level, neutrality, non-advice/UPL; (2) legal — primary-source citations present, attorney sign-off recorded, version-scoped; (3) accessibility — WCAG 2.2 AA spot-check, offline card legibility; (4) maintainer — CI green, schema valid, no secrets

Reviewers ledger format defined: records reviewer name, role, credentials (bar admission + jurisdiction for attorneys), consent to credit, and version-scoped sign-off (content version + citation hash)
Attorney sign-off is version-scoped: a later edit to content or its citations requires a fresh sign-off; the ledger format enforces this with a version/hash field

The workflow document specifies the attorney-veto rule: a licensed attorney's 'do not ship on substance' cannot be overridden by the maintainer; disagreements are logged and escalated to Elyos governance or a second attorney
Reviewed and approved by an independent editorial reviewer and the maintainer
output: A merged document PR containing the multi-gate review workflow specification, CODEOWNERS configuration, PR checklist template, and reviewers ledger format — making attorney-gated review a structural property of the repository, not a process hope.
